brief.name: ROUGH HOUSE
brief.industry: Independent Brooklyn bookstore + tiny publishing imprint (4 titles per year)
brief.audience: Readers in their 20s-30s tired of Amazon and BookTok algorithmic recommendation. They want to encounter writing they didn't already know to look for. Skeptical of corporate cultural curation. Buy 8-15 books per year, mostly fiction and essays.
brief.tone: confrontational, witty, opinionated, deliberately uncool, anti-curation, hand-made, alive
brief.references: Cards Against Humanity's homepage voice, McSweeney's, Are.na's UI, college punk-zine layouts, n+1 magazine, the original Strand Bookstore signage
brief.context: Hero should announce "WE SELL BOOKS WE LIKE." in massive type. Sections: Hero, The Shelves (5 curated categories with hand-written-feeling descriptions — NOT generic genres like "Fiction" or "Non-fiction"), Events (in-person readings and arguments, with dates), Press (the imprint side, 4 books per year with covers), Footer (address, phone, hours, "no parking available").
brief.avoid: Bestseller lists, "recommended for you" algorithmic copy, anything that sounds like Amazon or Goodreads, Instagram aesthetic, neutral typography, "literary" pretension, anything that uses the word "curated" sincerely.
